key_themes: Profit in line with consensus expectations (~£439m EBIT for FY26)
Capacity expansion (+8% Summer 2026 seats) and new London Gatwick base
Solid passenger growth (+7.9% booked) and booking momentum
Attractive valuation (low P/E, net cash position) with analyst Buy consensus
Sector pressures (fuel costs, winter market, promotions)
summary: Jet2 delivered a reassuring February 2026 trading update confirming FY26 operating profit is tracking market expectations of around £439 million, driven by strong capacity growth, new bases including Gatwick opening soon, and positive booking trends for its package holiday model.[[1]](https://www.reuters.com/business/uks-jet2-sees-profit-meeting-estimates-with-8-rise-summer-capacity-2026-02-25/)[[2]](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/news/jet2-profit-track-satisfied-summer-075700384.html) The shares have been volatile around the 1,200-1,240p level with a recent up day, viewed as undervalued by some investors citing low earnings multiples, net cash, and consistent sector outperformance, though heavy promotional emails have prompted limited skepticism on social media.[[3]](https://www.reddit.com/r/investingUK/comments/1snz7zj/jet2_trading_at_35x_net_income_is_this_the_most/)[[4]](https://www.investing.com/equities/dart-group-plc-consensus-estimates) Analysts maintain an overall Buy rating with average price targets implying ~30%+ upside; the April 29 year-end trading update will be a key near-term catalyst.[[5]](https://www.jet2plc.com/en/financial-calendar)
sources: 10+ news articles (Reuters, Yahoo Finance, Jet2 PLC releases on Feb 2026 trading update, Nov 2025 interims, Jul 2025 finals), X posts (~15 recent keyword/semantics results since Jan 2026; mostly consumer-focused with limited deep investor commentary), Analyst consensus (13 analysts: Buy rating, avg PT ~1645p), Investor forums (Reddit r/investingUK thread, ADVFN/LSE share chat references), Stock price data (recent ~1200-1240p range, +5% day on Apr 17 2026 but down from 2025 highs)
